question: Which year from 2011-2016 has the lowest teen birth rate for females ages 15-19 across all races in the United States? Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = 'datagov/nchs-teen-birth-rates-for-females-by-age-group-race-and-hispanic-origin-united-states/files/rows.txt'
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

df["Year"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Year"], errors="coerce")
df["Birth Rate"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Birth Rate"], errors="coerce")
filtered = df[df["Year"].between(2011, 2016) & df["Race or Hispanic Origin"].eq("All Races") & df["Age Group"].eq("15-19 Years")].copy()
ranked = filtered.sort_values(["Birth Rate", "Year"], ascending=[True, True], kind="mergesort")
answer = str(int(ranked.iloc[0]["Year"]))
1.subquestion: Which year from 2011-2016 has the lowest teen birth rate for females ages 15-19 across all races in the United States?

reasoning: Node 1: Filter to `Race or Hispanic Origin` = `All Races` and `Age Group` = `15-19 Years` within 2011-2016, then select the year with the smallest `Birth Rate`. The answer is 2016.
